description:
You are acting as a technical writer who is preparing a quick fact-sheet for developers.  
    Three log files already exist inside the directory  
        /home/user/project/logs/  
    and each file contains normal timestamp-free lines that may start with words such
    as “INFO”, “WARN”, or “ERROR”.

    Your job:
      1. Count how many **lines** (not individual words) contain the exact, 
         case-sensitive string “ERROR” across **all files whose names end with “.log”**
         that are located directly inside /home/user/project/logs/  
         (do **not** descend into sub-directories, and do not consider files that do
         not have the “.log” suffix).
      2. Produce a one-line summary file named  
            /home/user/project/docs/error_summary.txt  
         that contains exactly the following format (no extra spaces or blank lines):

            TOTAL_ERROR_LINES:<number>

         Replace <number> with the integer you found.  The line must be terminated
         with exactly one Unix newline character “\n”.  Nothing else may appear in
         the file.
      3. Once the file is written, display its contents on the terminal so that the
         automated checker can read it.  (For example, you could `cat` the file after
         you have created it.)

    Hints & constraints:
      • Accomplishing steps 1–2 can be done with a single well-crafted shell
        pipeline command, but you may use extra commands if you wish.  
      • You do not need sudo or root privileges.  
      • If /home/user/project/docs/ does not yet exist, create it with normal user
        permissions before writing the summary file.  
      • Make sure the summary file content matches the format above *exactly*; the
        automated test will perform a strict comparison.

    End state that the grader will verify:
      • The file /home/user/project/docs/error_summary.txt exists, is readable,
        and contains precisely one line in the format shown above followed by
        a single newline.  
      • The integer reported must equal the total number of lines containing the
        string “ERROR” in the three existing *.log files inside
        /home/user/project/logs/.
truth:
The grading system will ensure the following files already exist before the
    agent begins:

    1. /home/user/project/logs/app.log  
       Content (each line is terminated with a single “\n”):
           INFO Application started
           WARN Cache miss
           ERROR Failed to load config
           INFO Shutdown complete

       — This file contributes 1 “ERROR” line.

    2. /home/user/project/logs/db.log  
       Content:
           INFO Connection established
           ERROR Query timeout
           ERROR Could not commit
           INFO Connection closed

       — This file contributes 2 “ERROR” lines.

    3. /home/user/project/logs/auth.log  
       Content:
           INFO User login
           WARN Password attempt
           ERROR Invalid token
           ERROR Session expired
           ERROR Permission denied
           INFO Logout

       — This file contributes 3 “ERROR” lines.

    Therefore the correct total that must appear in
    /home/user/project/docs/error_summary.txt is:

        TOTAL_ERROR_LINES:6\n

    The grader will:
      • Verify that the file exists at the exact path specified.
      • Verify that its sole content is exactly the 22 characters:
            "TOTAL_ERROR_LINES:6\n"
        (19 visible characters + the digit 6 + one newline).
      • Verify there are no extra spaces, carriage returns, or additional lines.
